PicFast
Docs GitHub 中文
Open Source · MIT License

Your images.
Your server.
Your control.

Deploy a full-featured image hosting service in minutes. No tracking, no limits, no third-party dependency.

terminal 
$ docker run -d -p 8080:8080 \
  -v ./data:/data \
  picfast/picfast
Get Started View on GitHub

Everything you need, nothing you don't

API-First Design

Clean REST API with OpenAPI spec. Upload via curl, ShareX, or any HTTP client. Integrate with your existing workflow — no vendor lock-in.

Privacy by Default

No tracking, no analytics, no ads. Your images stay on your server. Optional content moderation — you control what gets hosted.

Multiple Storage Backends

Local filesystem, S3, OSS, COS, Kodo, or WebDAV. Switch storage backends without re-uploading existing images.

Developer Experience

OpenAPI documentation with live testing, ShareX support, multi-format link output (URL, Markdown, BBCode, HTML).

Extensible

Group-based permission system, configurable upload limits, image processing (compression, watermarking, thumbnail generation).

Modern Stack

Built with Go and React. Single binary deployment. PostgreSQL for persistence, no external service dependencies.

How it compares

Chevereto Photoview Self-built S3 PicFast
Setup time ~30 min ~20 min ~2 hours ~5 min
API support Basic Limited Custom OpenAPI + docs
ShareX integration ✅ ❌ Custom ✅ Built-in
Storage backends Limited Local only S3 only 6 backends
Content moderation ❌ ❌ Custom ✅ Built-in
Thumbnail generation ✅ ✅ Custom ✅ Auto
Image processing Basic Limited Custom ✅ Compression + watermark
Open source Paid ✅ N/A ✅ MIT

Quick start

1

Pull the image

One command to get started. Data persists in a local volume.

docker run -d -p 8080:8080 \
  -v $(pwd)/data:/data \
  picfast/picfast
2

Configure

Edit config.yaml to set your domain, storage, and admin credentials.

cp config.example.yaml config.yaml
$EDITOR config.yaml
3

Upload via API

Upload your first image with a simple curl command.

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/upload \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" \
  -F "file=@image.png"

Full documentation →

PicFast

Open source, self-hosted image hosting
